headings.usage: Team Name, Player Numbers, Large Counters

body.usage: Player Names, Subtitles, UI elements, Settings

visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: Bench drawer uses high-end glass (bg-black/80 with backdrop-blur-2xl) and a sharp 1px top border (border-white/10) to separate it from the pitch.
visual_enhancers.textures: Pitch uses alternating green stripes via CSS repeating-linear-gradient to simulate mowed grass. Very subtle to not distract from the players.
visual_enhancers.shadows: Players on the pitch have drop-shadow-2xl to float above the grass, increasing contrast.

layouts.app: Strictly 100dvh, flex column. Prevent overscroll on mobile body.
layouts.header: Minimal. Height ~60px. Flex row, space-between. Shows 'Östra Squad' and Settings icon.
layouts.pitch_area: Flex-1 relative container with generous padding. The actual pitch should have margin inside to prevent players from overlapping the absolute screen edges.
layouts.bench: Fixed at the bottom. Height ~110px. Horizontal scrolling flex container for substitute players. overflow-x-auto, hide scrollbar. Items gap-4.

components.name: Pitch
components.description: Vertical football field. Requires relative positioning. Inner absolute elements for center line, center circle, top/bottom penalty areas, and goals.

components.name: Player
components.description: Draggable component. Round avatar (w-12 h-12) with object-cover, white border. A small black circle badge at bottom-right containing the player number in white.

components.name: Bench
components.description: Bottom glass panel. Houses players where isOnPitch === false.

components.name: SettingsDialog
components.description: Shadcn Dialog to select target number of players on the pitch (e.g., 5, 7, 9, 11).

motion.drag: Crucial: Player elements must be seamlessly draggable. Add visual feedback (scale: 1.15, zIndex: 50, cursor: grabbing) while dragging.
motion.interactions: Smooth layout transitions when players move between the bench and the pitch.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build a mobile-first web app for 'Östra Squad'. Target iOS touch interactions natively.

Recommend using `@dnd-kit/core` with `useDraggable` and `useDroppable` or a unified `framer-motion` layout approach. Dragging out of a horizontally scrollable container (Bench) onto a free-form absolute canvas (Pitch) can be tricky. @dnd-kit handles portals and scroll containers well. If using framer-motion, ensure the dragged element doesn't get clipped by the bench's overflow-hidden/overflow-x-auto.
Generate mock data for 24 players. Give them youth-sounding names, numbers (1-24), and placeholder avatar images (cycle through the provided urls).

Implement the pitch design using raw CSS + Tailwind. A green rectangle with a subtle alternating stripe effect. Inside: a horizontal white line at 50% height. A white circle dead center. Two white boxes at the top and bottom for penalty areas. Use border-2 border-white/80.
Header should display the team name and a counter 'X / Y Players' (where Y is chosen in settings). Highlight the counter if X > Y.
There should be a 'Settings' icon in the header that opens a dialog (Shadcn UI) to set 'Number of players on pitch' (e.g., 5, 7, 9, 11).
Ensure the entire app is wrapped in a container with `h-[100dvh] w-screen overflow-hidden bg-[#050505] text-white` to prevent standard mobile scrolling. Add `touch-action: none` to draggable players.

Player icons: w-12 h-12 rounded-full border-2 border-white bg-black object-cover. The player number must be a small badge (w-5 h-5 bg-black rounded-full text-[10px] font-bold flex items-center justify-center absolute -bottom-1 -right-1 z-10).
Bench should support horizontal scrolling. Players should look like neat little circles with their numbers.
When a player is dropped on the Pitch, their position must be absolutely positioned based on the drop coordinates relative to the Pitch container.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You MUST NOT apply universal transition like `transition: all`.
You MUST NOT center align the app container in CSS.
Use named exports for components and default exports for pages.
All interactive elements MUST have data-testid attributes.
Do NOT use generic 'blue' primary colors. The app is Black, Green, White.
CRITICAL: Avoid transparent backgrounds on the settings dialog overlay; ensure proper backdrop blur.
